creator: fuz@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Alexey Dokuchaev from comment #1)

It's kind of crazy that after decades of egrep being fine it's suddenly supposed to be not fine anymore.  This whole nagging thing is ridiculous.  It even breaks some of my unit tests (which get confused by the extra stderr output).

What's the rationale behind this?  Do you think they're actually going to remove the egrep and fgrep scripts?  I honestly just don't see the point.  They have always existed and removing them is just going to break a lot of scripts.  It boggles the mind how a mature open source project can decide to deprecate one of the major ways people use it after decades of everything being fine and dandy.  Is there perhaps a new maintainer who wants to earn his stripes?

My position: if you install the scripts, they should not nag you.  Otherwise don't install the scripts, that's fine, too with me (as we already provide egrep and fgrep in base).  It may not be fine with other users though.
